name: Swiss Standard German (Hochdeutsch)
notes: Swiss Standard German (Schweizer Hochdeutsch). NO glottal stop /ʔ/ — absent from Swiss German phonology. /r/ = alveolar trill [r] (vs. Standard German uvular [ʀ]). Geminate consonants preserved as distinct (Alemannic feature). Swiss German DIALECTS (Schweizerdeutsch) are Alemannic and much more divergent than Swiss Standard German. Source: König & van der Auwera (1994).

sources.author: Christen, H.
sources.title: Convergenz und Divergenz in der schweizerdeutschen Gegenwartsdialekte
